calibration.calibration_note: With hazard_harm=0.02, avg episode ~182 steps (was ~18 with 0.5). Actual episode time higher.

items.title: MECH-269b symmetric V_s gating on E1/E2 cortical rollouts (Q-040 factorial) -- re-queue after external SIGTERM
items.note: Re-queue of V3-EXQ-490 after external SIGTERM (exit code -15) on ree-cloud-2 at 109/320 min on 2026-04-27T03:38:27Z. Not a code crash: runner subprocess was killed externally (no traceback, no output file, runner stayed alive and ran V3-EXQ-493 two seconds later; no force_stop entry in runner_commands/ree-cloud-2.json). Most likely cause: shared-vCPU CX22 OOM neighbour pressure or transient cloud scheduler kill. Script unchanged from V3-EXQ-490 -- no fix needed; this entry just retries. Same Q-040 factorial diagnostic (ON_OFF vs ON_ON arms, only manipulated variable is use_vs_rollout_gating; C1 gate fires > 0 holds, C2 ON approach_commit_count > 0 in >= 2/3 seeds, C3 ON dacc_score_bias_mean > 0). claim_ids unchanged from predecessor (MECH-269b primary; Q-040 the open question this factorial answers). machine_affinity any per user instruction.

items.title: SD-039 anchor goal-snapshot payload population layer validation (6 sub-tests)
items.note: Diagnostic validation of the SD-039 module-level write-site population layer landed 2026-04-27. Six sub-tests UC1-UC6: UC1 substrate classes accessible, UC2 master OFF bit-identical (no payload, empty query), UC3 population fires under master ON + active goal regime (anchors carry populated payload, query returns non-zero goal_match), UC4 dual-trace preservation across mark_inactive (inactive anchors retain payload), UC5 falsifiable signature -- goal-active vs goal-inactive phase mean goal_match dissociation (Phase A < 0.05 vs Phase B > 0.3), UC6 MECH-094 simulation gate (replay-path writes do NOT populate payload). Forces MECH-288 fast-scale boundary every 8 ticks via event_segmenter.force_boundary so the SD-039 contract is exercised without depending on stochastic boundary firing. Behavioural ghost-goal validation (does goal-match-aware querying improve recovery from blocked goals?) lives in MECH-292 / MECH-293 successor experiments. Mac smoke 2026-04-27: 6/6 PASS (~23s).
